channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Agents need vector search more than RAG ever did
item.description:
What's the role of vector databases in the agentic AI world? That's a question that organizations have been coming to terms with in recent months.
The narrative had real momentum. As large language models scaled to million-token context windows, a credible argument circulated among enterprise architects: purpose-built vector search was a stopgap, not infrastructure. Agentic memory would absorb the retrieval problem. Vector databases were a RAG-era artifact.The production evidence is running the other way.Qdrant, the Berlin-based open source vector search company, announced a $50 million Serie...

item.title: A ‘Super El Niño’ Could Be Coming—Here’s What That Means for You
item.description: Climate models suggest this year could bring one of the strongest El Niño events on record, driving more extreme weather and smashing global temperature records.

item.title: 'It's been 20 years, it's surprising to me how little has changed': Sonos CEO and former Pandora exec Tom Conrad reveals what he thinks is 'holding us back' from more music streaming innovation
item.description: 'No one is motivated by making an innovative music discovery experience'

item.title: When an Anime Can’t Quite Live Up to Its Incredible Trailer
item.description: A fleeting glimpse of 'Origin: Spirits of the Past' had no business being a spiritual experience outshining 'Dragon Ball Z.'

item.title: Channel Surfer lets you watch YouTube like it’s old-school cable TV
item.description: The web app lets you turn on YouTube and surf channels like you're watching cable TV in the '90s.

item.title: Why Rivian is holding the $45,000 base model R2 until ‘late 2027’
item.description: Rivian spent the last two years promoting the R2 as a $45,000 SUV. But buyers won't be able to pay that price until late next year -- if at all.

item.title: Palantir CEO Insists He Doesn’t Support Regime Change Wars (But Supports Iran War)
item.description: "No one believes it, but Palantir is the most important protector of the Fourth Amendment..." said Alex Karp.

item.title: John Solly Is the DOGE Operative Accused of Planning to Take Social Security Data to His New Job
item.description: A whistleblower complaint alleges John Solly claimed to have stored highly sensitive Social Security data on a thumb drive. Solly and Leidos, his current employer, strongly deny the allegations.

item.title: M5 MacBook Air Review: A Laptop With Real Middle Child Energy
item.description: Apple's thinnest laptop is powerful for most tasks. Still, there may be better options above and below it.

item.title: Your Corporate Jargon–Loving Coworker Might Actually Be as Stupid as You Think, Study Shows
item.description: We all know that guy, and yes, your suspicions are true: Advocates of corporate speech aren't the sharpest pencils in the office.

item.title: Honda Cancels Plans for Three News EVs as It Scrambles to Rethink Strategy
item.description: The company says shifting U.S. policies and rising competition from Chinese EV makers forced it to scrap three upcoming EV models.

item.title: RAMaggedon not expected to ease this year as IDC cuts 2026 PC market forecast again
item.description: We've been seeing all sorts of warnings about how RAMaggedon is nigh. The latest horseman signalling a disaster is the International Data Corporation, which had already cautioned that things were looking bad at the end of 2025. Today, the organization further cut its forecasts for the PC market in 2026, anticipating that global shipments would fall 11.6 percent. The previous report projected that this year would see a falloff of up to 8.9 percent due to ongoing memory shortages. And the new figure was set before the escalation of conflicts in Iran and across the Middle East, which could furthe...

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters is officially getting a sequel
item.description: KPop Demon Hunters is getting a sequel, Netflix and Sony have announced. Sony Pictures Animation handed the rights to the film to Netflix in 2021 as part of a larger licensing deal, but neither company could have expected how much of a hit it would ultimately become. Besides being Netflix's "most-watched movie of all time," KPop Demon Hunters is also nominated for Best Animated Feature and Best Original Song at the 98th Academy Awards, and stands a good chance of winning.Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, the directors of the first film, are returning to direct the sequel. The project will be th...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Anthropic’s DOD Lawsuit, War Memes, and AI Coming for VC Jobs
item.description: In today’s episode, we discuss how the saga between Anthropic and the Department of Defense is far from over.

item.title: ‘Starfleet Academy’ Puts Itself on Trial, and Finds Its Old Generations Wanting
item.description: 'Rubincon' ends the first season of 'Starfleet Academy' with an interesting twist on its belief in the next generation—with some repuditation of the ones that came before them.

item.title: Researchers Say AI Is Homogenizing Human Expression and Thought
item.description: For a "large" language model, the diversity of outputs is pretty small.

item.title: Buy now or wait? SD card prices are shooting through the roof – here’s how to navigate the ‘memory crisis’
item.description: A global 'memory crisis' has seen the price of SD cards shoot up in 2026, typically by over 200%. Here's my buying advice, and which genuine models I still recommend

item.title: Trader Joe’s is bringing back its viral mini tote bags. Here’s when to start looking
item.description: The miniature canvas totes will return in delicate pink, baby blue, mint green, and lavender—the same pastel shades shoppers snapped up last year. Nothing says springtime like a canvas tote drop from Trader Joe’s. That’s right. The highly anticipated shopping bags are back and ready to fly off the shelves (and, probably, the resale sites) once again. ...

item.title: Tinder drops a slew of updates, including an AI way to cut through dating fatigue
item.description: Tinder announces several features and product updates, including the Chemistry AI feature and Events.

item.title: What it was like to watch grieving parents stare down Mark Zuckerberg in court
item.description: Around a dozen parents huddled in the dim hallway outside the courtroom in February, nervously gripping paper tickets. They were glaring at a gray tote bag held by a member of the court staff - the one who'd determine, by lottery, if they made it inside. Pinned on bags and coats, butterfly clips honored children […]

item.title: Save £150 on this 32GB Asus Core 7 mini PC in Amazon’s Spring Sale
item.description: Save over £150 on this Asus Core 7 mini PC with 32GB DDR5 and Windows 11 Pro in Amazon's Spring Deal Days sale

item.title: The original AirTag is the cheapest it’s ever been
item.description: Despite the fact Apple released a new AirTag in January, the first-generation AirTag is still a top-notch tracker if you’re embedded in Apple’s ecosystem. And right now, it’s on sale for $13.91 ($15.09 off) at Walmart, which marks a new low price. If you’re an iPhone owner, the original AirTag still delivers a level of […]

item.title: Amazon Employees Say AI Is Just Increasing Workload. A New Study Confirms Their Suspicions
item.description: Silicon Valley promised AI would make work less intense. Employees would beg to differ.

item.title: Meta is testing clickable links in Instagram captions for verified subscribers
item.description: Instagram has long limited users' ability to share links, restricting link-sharing to Stories, Reels and user profiles. But that might now be changing. The company has started to test clickable links inside of post captions for subscribers to Meta Verified. The new feature, which has been a long-requested update from creators, was spotted by blogger Andrea Valeria, who posted screenshots of a clickable Substack link she was able to add to an Instagram post. According to Valeria, an in-app message indicated she could share up to 10 links a month.Meta confirmed to Engadget that it's testing link...

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters is getting a sequel, obviously
item.description: Get ready to get even more K-pop, demonier, and as huntery as possible. Netflix's smash-hit, Oscar-nominated animated film KPop Demon Hunters is returning for a sequel, with the fictional Korean girl group HUNTR/X coming back to lay down pop hits and smash evil boy bands (and/or other demons). Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans will once […]

item.title: PEGI ratings for game releases in Europe will be age-restricted if they contain loot boxes
item.description: European regulators are continuing to crack down on loot boxes and gaming features it classifies as "interactive risk categories." The Pan-European Game Information, better known as PEGI, is rolling out new rules that will apply age ratings based on the presence of loot boxes and other in-game purchases or systems that could be tied to gambling or addictive behavior. The exact policies are as follows:Purchases of in-game content: games with time-limited or quantity-limited offers will be classified with a PEGI 12, games with NFTs or blockchain-related mechanisms will be PEGI 18.Paid random ite...
